From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Annual figure skating competition held in 1902

| 1902 World Figure Skating Championships | |
| --- | --- |
| Type: | ISU Championship |
| Date: | February 13 |
| Season: | 1902 |
| Location: | [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [London](/wiki/London "London"), [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") |
| Champions | |
| Men's singles:   [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") [Ulrich Salchow](/wiki/Ulrich_Salchow "Ulrich Salchow") | |
| Navigation | |
| Previous:   [1901 World Championships](/wiki/1901_World_Figure_Skating_Championships "1901 World Figure Skating Championships") | |
| Next:   [1903 World Championships](/wiki/1903_World_Figure_Skating_Championships "1903 World Figure Skating Championships") | |

The **[World Figure Skating Championships](/wiki/World_Figure_Skating_Championships "World Figure Skating Championships")** is an annual [figure skating competition](/wiki/Figure_skating_competition "Figure skating competition") sanctioned by the [International Skating Union](/wiki/International_Skating_Union "International Skating Union") in which [figure skaters](/wiki/Figure_skating "Figure skating") compete for the title of World Champion.

The competition took place on 13 February in [London](/wiki/London "London"), [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"). It was the first World Championships held on artificial ice.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

It was assumed that only men would compete in the event, and just four skaters participated. However, one of them was a woman, [Madge Syers](/wiki/Madge_Syers "Madge Syers"). After the competition, the winner, [Ulrich Salchow](/wiki/Ulrich_Salchow "Ulrich Salchow"), delighted with Syers, presented her with the gold medal he had just won.[[2]](#cite_note-2) At the time, the International Skating Union rules did not specify that only men are allowed to participate. In the following year, rules were changed and separate championships for females and males were introduced. However, it was several years before the ladies' event first took place in 1906.

## Results

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1902_World_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Results")]

### Singles

| **2** | [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Madge Syers](/wiki/Madge_Syers "Madge Syers") | 20 | 2 | 843 | 3 | 396 | 1239 | 247.8 | 13 |

* [![Russian Empire](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/f/f3/Flag_of_Russia.svg/40px-Flag_of_Russia.svg.png)](/wiki/Russian_Empire "Russian Empire") Piotr Weryho
* [![Sweden](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/Swedish_civil_ensign_%281844%E2%80%931905%29.svg/40px-Swedish_civil_ensign_%281844%E2%80%931905%29.svg.png)](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") Ivar Westergren

### Pairs (unofficial)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1902_World_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Pairs (unofficial)")]

The 1902 championships were the first to hold a pairs competition alongside the singles. The title of "world champions" and medals were not awarded, since the only pair to enter were [![United Kingdom](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a5/Flag_of_the_United_Kingdom_%281-2%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_Kingdom_%281-2%29.svg.png)](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Madge Syers](/wiki/Madge_Syers "Madge Syers") / [Edgar Syers](/wiki/Edgar_Syers "Edgar Syers").[[3]](#cite_note-3)
